what_it_does: A living build plan that defines the front-interface architecture as an addressed-state renderer, assigns section ownership to fabric parts, lists seam contracts, and sets a first end-to-end slice.
observations: Defines the front interface as a rendered projection of one addressed state, navigated by addressing rather than as a static screen.
Locks four navigation axes: scale/hierarchy, time/sequence, frame/perspective, state/phase, and maps each to an interface gesture.
Declares a two-call spine: fork.project(address) produces structured territory, composition.resolve(address) renders live DOM.
Claims an existing live projection engine (bridge.build_projection / mcp__company__project) should be reused rather than rebuilt.
Sections are owned by named channels/parts: composition, fork, DNA, recollection, wildcard, lead.
Includes explicit seam contracts between parts, framed as file-disjoint meet-points.
Status markers show some sections contributed and others pending or provisional.
First slice targets board://<id> end-to-end through project() and resolve().
Uses dense project-specific jargon and references session transcripts and channel IDs.
standouts: The reuse-don't-parallel directive: the spine already exists as bridge.build_projection.
The four axes are explicitly mapped to interface gestures (zoom, history/time-travel, identity/look, current condition/cycle).
The two-call spine and meet-point contract between fork and composition.
Wildcard's Circuit loop adds write-back, recognition-surface, and collapse-κ rendering to a read path.
conventions: Living-document style with dates, owner tags, and provisional markers.
Channel-ID and transcript-ID citations as provenance.
Heavy use of internal neologisms and metaphors (THE CIRCUIT, THE SPINE, collapse-κ, CAND-13).
Explicit seam contracts as file-disjoint meet-points.
First-slice / GO milestones for incremental validation.
concerns: Heavy jargon and project-specific references may impede readability outside the immediate team.
Several sections are marked pending or provisional, so the plan is incomplete.
The relationship between composition.resolve and the existing bridge.build_projection is left as an open reconciliation question.
Dense abstraction may obscure concrete implementation details.
Multiple first slices mentioned across sections could create integration ordering ambiguity.
notes: A coordination-heavy build plan that tries to prevent a second projection engine by declaring a shared addressed-state spine and four-axis navigation contract.
questions: What is the exact schema of contracts.address?
How does bridge.build_projection map to DOM rendering versus geometry-field rendering?
What concrete state schema does fork's project() output use?
What is the precise definition and implementation of collapse-κ?
How do DNA identity packs get authored and loaded?
What is the relationship between the board slice and the existing small store?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a build plan for the front interface; the file fulfills that and also acts as a cross-part integration contract, so it is slightly broader than a pure build plan.
apparent_themes: Reuse existing substrate rather than duplicate it.
Collapse and provenance as visible state.
intention_signals: It is trying to converge multiple parallel subsystem efforts into a single addressed-state interface by declaring a shared resolver, four navigation axes, and explicit seams, while preventing a second implementation of the projection engine.
novelty: The addressed-state circuit model where every rendered element carries a sub-address and human recognition writes back mutations at that sub-address, combined with four-axis navigation.
contribution: A high-level integration contract and first-slice roadmap that names the shared address grammar, the two-call spine, the four navigation axes, and the reuse directive for the live projection engine.
summary_for_embedding: A build plan defining the front interface as an addressed-state renderer with a two-call project/resolve spine, four navigation axes, part-owned sections, seam contracts, and a reuse directive for an existing live projection engine.
intention_for_embedding: To coordinate the fabric's front-interface build by locking a shared addressed-state architecture and preventing duplicate resolver work.
